query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed the research on the 2026 Johor state election. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Election:** 11 July 2026, all 56 seats contested (majority = 29), ~2.7M voters. Called ~1 year early by caretaker MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi (BN).

**Expected outcome — BN retention near-certain:** Analysts describe a BN win as "almost a foregone conclusion"; the debate is only about the margin. Ong Kian Ming's most-likely (pro-BN, >60%) scenario projects **BN 53, PH 3, PN 0**. His pro-PN scenario is rated <10%.

**Green wave / PN & Malay voters:** The PN "green wave" that surged among conservative Malay voters in recent elections is significantly undercut here by a **bitter PAS–Bersatu split**. Critically, PN's Johor base is anchored by Bersatu (not PAS), so PAS–BN vote-transfer understandings are unreliable — a key reason PN's upside is rated low.

**BN incumbency / Onn Hafiz:** He's a popular, technocratic MB and BN's campaign centerpiece; Johor secured record RM110bn (~US$25.7bn) investment in 2025. Risks: early-poll gambit and royal activism in candidate selection/tax disputes.

**PH is weak:** No named MB candidate, top leaders mostly not contesting, no cohesive narrative, and base drained by the new Bersama party (Rafizi Ramli).

**Caveat:** Public polling is scarce and multi-cornered fights (only 14 straight fights vs. 42 multi-way races) plus turnout of Johor workers returning from Singapore introduce genuine seat-level uncertainty. A dominant BN win is expected to spill into Negeri Sembilan (1 Aug) and could trigger GE16 in 2026. Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election
sources.snippet: Expected to take place on 11 July 2026; dissolution announced 1 June 2026 by Onn Hafiz Ghazi; all 56 seats contested.

sources.title: 2022 Johor state election
sources.snippet: BN won 40 seats and a two-thirds majority; PH won 12; PN won 3; MUDA won 1.

sources.title: Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are double trouble for Anwar
sources.snippet: Johor campaign centred on strong-performing MB Onn Hafiz; RM110bn investment in 2025; PN can tap conservative Malay sentiment but is openly divided.

sources.title: Opinion: Projecting the Johor state election results
sources.snippet: Pro-BN scenario (>60%): BN 53, PH 3. Pro-PN (<10%): PN 25, BN 21, PH 10. Pro-PH: BN 39, PH 14, PN 3.

sources.title: Nomination day for an unpredictable Johor State Election completed
sources.snippet: 56 seats, no walkovers; 14 straight, 27 three-cornered, 12 four-way, 3 five-way; ~2.7m voters; media BN-sweep narrative under-supported by data.

sources.title: Johor Polls: BN, PH Lead Field With 56 Candidates Each
sources.snippet: BN and PH contest all 56 seats; PN contests 33; Bersama 15; MUDA 4; PSM 1; ASLI debut in Endau.

sources.title: The 2026 Johor Election: Advantage Barisan Nasional?
sources.snippet: BN called polls a year early with momentum but some risk; Onn Hafiz and BN have wind in their sails.

sources.title: PAS-BN electoral play in 23 seats faces Bersatu wildcard
sources.snippet: PN support in Johor anchored by Bersatu not PAS; after PAS-Bersatu split, vote transfers to BN unreliable.

sources.title: Former DAP Analyst Sees BN Sweeping Johor, He Also Sees What Comes Next
sources.snippet: Ong Kian Ming projects BN 53 of 56, PH 3, PN 0; result could dissolve Parliament and trigger GE16.

sources.title: PAS hedging bets on Bersatu for Johor polls
sources.snippet: PAS not rushing into complete organisational split with Bersatu, its PN co-founder.

sources.title: Final tally for Johor polls is BN 40, Pakatan 11, Perikatan 3, PKR 1, Muda 1, says EC
sources.snippet: Official 2022 results: BN 40, PH 11, PN 3, PKR 1, MUDA 1.
